files.preview: Bayesian Bodybuilding 2014  1    Schedule   Your first meal of the day should be when you naturally get hungry on most days.  This is usually a few hours after waking up. Don’t forcefeed your breakfast, but don’t  go hungry either.   Keep your daily meal frequency constant: always eat 4 meals at roughly the same  times every day.    It is important to always train at roughly the same time, so find a time to train when  you are sure that you can train in between your shifts. files.rel_path: Case study classic bodybuilding peak week.pdf
files.preview: CLASSIC BODYBUILDING PEAK WEEK    This document contains a case study of the peak week protocol of one of Menno’s natural classic  bodybuilding clients. It’s reasonably representative for how most peak week protocols for men’s  bodybuilding classes end up. Importantly, this is the finalized version of half a dozen trial runs to  optimize the carbload intake, figure out that cutting water wasn’t worth it as long as sodium intake  was limited, and find the ideal shitload foods.    Days 1-3

files.preview: Setting Calories: Step by Step  Bayesian Method    Because BMR is a function of LBM, we need to know BF% and LBM to be most accurate.  1. LBM = BW x (1 - BF% / 100): LBM= 55.90(1 - 14.4%/100) = 47.85kg         If no DEXA, Estimate BF% to find LBM. Find BMI. BMI = Wt (kg) / Ht (m2)  For Untrained: BF% = [1.2 x BMI] + [0.23 x Age] – [10.8 x Sex m=1, f=0] – 5.4  2. Katch McArdle BMR = 370 + (21.6 x LBM in kg):  1404      3. Conservatively estimate the daily life physical activity level (PA):   1.

files.preview: Case study: training at home  What if a client trains at home and only has access to barbells and dumbbells? Exercise  selection can then change as follows.    Chest: Dumbbells will have to be used for the fly. Bands and chains would be great here. If  someone doesn't have them and their shoulders act up during DB flies, I like to use fly-presses:  it's basically a fly movement at the top that turns into a press in the bottom position.
